g.text: linear interpolation — so the true velocity is CONSTANT along the path and
per step: draw x₀ from data, ε from N(0,I), σ from a schedule —
σ sampling is the main tuning knob: uniform is the baseline; logit-normal
(SD3) concentrates training where the task is hardest — mid noise. The σ
also conditions the net (AdaLN / time embedding).
relation to diffusion: DDPM's ε-prediction, x₀-prediction and v-prediction are the
same regression with different output parameterizations and σ-weightings —
rectified flow picks the straight path + velocity target.
Euler steps along the learned field. Because the target path is straight, few
steps go far (4-30 typical; distilled models: 1-4). Same loop powers image,
One straight path between data and noise; the network learns its constant velocity. The recipe behind SD3, FLUX and Wan.
